description: Pinned Lambda-benchmark targets (issue #110). Each target dispatches ONE shard -- the densest cell over the NEON SERC AOP box -- so cost/runtime deltas track code changes, not data drift. Shard maps are shared per (grid, order): the densest shard does not depend on the aggregator. Rebuild + drift check: tests/test_benchmark_shardmap.py.

healpix_o11.note: 4-way tie at 44 granules; the lowest-key tiebreak makes the pin deterministic. The benchmark always dispatches this static shard_key, so the series is stable regardless of CMR drift. The phase-5 drift test must tolerate tie reselection (compare granule count within +/-1, not exact shard_key).

rect_3km.note: Rectilinear (UTM 18N) ~order-11 analog: 3 km shard, 9 km2. Built with the exact-S2 spherely backend. 4-way tie at 44 granules; lowest-key tiebreak -> shard 0. Drift test is tie-tolerant (granule count, not exact key).

rect_6km.note: Rectilinear (UTM 18N) ~order-10 analog: 6 km shard, 36 km2. 2-way tie at 47 granules; lowest-key tiebreak -> shard 0.

provisional_targets._comment: PROVISIONAL, PR-TREE-ONLY (issue #130, @espg decision https://github.com/englacial/zagg/issues/130#issuecomment-4838186259). These three scalar targets share ONE config + ONE shard (healpix_o11) and differ only by handoff (pandas / arrow / arrow-kernel) to benchmark the per-cell carrier cost and the experimental kernel reducer. They are deliberately NOT in 'targets' so they do NOT join the committed every-merge matrix until the kernel keep/drop decision is made. run_benchmark.py runs them by explicit --target name (via /benchmark); 'run all' (no --target) skips them.
